title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Execution Trace: Feed Go Retention Deletion
summary: Security is split across ad hoc checks, admin paths bypass URL policy, XML and HTML have different budgets, search supports denial of service, secrets enter logs, and data is kept forever. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set purpose-bound retention for raw responses, content revisions, logs, reading state, accounts, backups, legal holds, and deletion verification.

For Feed aggregation and Go Defend the Feed Go Retention Deletion model · Theory Feed Go Retention Dele…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Feed content is public, so retention and deletion do not matter.
